examples.title: DPDA Transition Function Representation
examples.description: Data structure to represent DPDA transition function as a dictionary
examples.explanation: Transition function stored as nested dictionary mapping (state, input, stack_top) to (next_state, action). Action is string to push (ε means pop). Enables O(1) lookup during simulation.

examples.title: DPDA Simulation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to simulate DPDA on input string and determine acceptance
examples.code:
FUNCTION simulate_DPDA(input_string):
 state = q0 // Initial state
 stack = ['Z0'] // Stack with bottom marker
 input_pos = 0 // Current input position
 
 WHILE input_pos < len(input_string) OR has_epsilon_transitions:
 // Try epsilon transitions first (deterministic order)
 IF has_epsilon_transition(state, stack[-1]):
 state, action = get_epsilon_transition(state, stack[-1])
 apply_stack_action(action)
 CONTINUE
 
 // Try input symbol transition
 symbol = input_string[input_pos]
 IF has_transition(state, symbol, stack[-1]):
 state, action = get_transition(state, symbol, stack[-1])
 apply_stack_action(action)
 input_pos += 1
 CONTINUE
 
 // No valid transition - reject
 RETURN REJECT
 
 // Input consumed, check acceptance
 IF stack is empty: // Empty stack acceptance
 RETURN ACCEPT
 ELSE IF state in F: // Final state acceptance
 RETURN ACCEPT
 ELSE:
 RETURN REJECT

FUNCTION apply_stack_action(action):
 IF action == 'ε': // Pop
 stack.pop()
 ELSE: // Push action (e.g., 'aZ0')
 stack.pop() // Pop current top
 FOR ch IN reverse(action): // Push new symbols
 stack.append(ch)
examples.explanation: Simulates DPDA deterministically. At each step, tries ε-transitions before input transitions (or follows defined order). Rejects if no valid transition. Accepts by empty stack or final state based on DPDA type.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × k) where n = input length, k = max stack operations

examples.title: Check Determinism of PDA
examples.description: Algorithm to verify if a given PDA is deterministic
examples.code:
FUNCTION is_deterministic_PDA(PDA):
 FOR each state q IN PDA.states:
 FOR each stack_symbol X IN PDA.stack_alphabet:
 // Check: if there's an input transition, no epsilon on same X
 has_input_transition = FALSE
 has_epsilon_transition = FALSE
 
 FOR each input a IN PDA.input_alphabet:
 IF PDA.transitions[(q, a, X)] is defined:
 has_input_transition = TRUE
 
 IF PDA.transitions[(q, ε, X)] is defined:
 has_epsilon_transition = TRUE
 
 // Determinism condition violated
 IF has_input_transition AND has_epsilon_transition:
 RETURN FALSE // Not deterministic
 
 // Check: at most one transition per (q, a, X)
 FOR each input a IN PDA.input_alphabet:
 count = number of transitions from (q, a, X)
 IF count > 1:
 RETURN FALSE // Not deterministic
 
 RETURN TRUE // PDA is deterministic
examples.explanation: Verifies determinism conditions: (1) No simultaneous ε and input transitions on same stack symbol, (2) At most one transition per (state, input, stack-top). Returns TRUE if PDA satisfies all conditions.
